claim: TeammateRpc.stop() cannot execute its SIGKILL fallback. After sending SIGTERM it immediately nulls this.proc, and the timer also checks proc.killed, which Node sets true once a signal was sent, not once the process exited. A stubborn child can survive stop()/kill/shutdown indefinitely.
evidence: teammate-rpc.ts:188-192 sends SIGTERM, schedules `if (this.proc && !this.proc.killed) this.proc.kill("SIGKILL")`, then immediately sets `this.proc = null`. Per Node docs, ChildProcess.killed only means a signal was successfully delivered, not that the child terminated.
runtimeTrigger: A worker process wedges or ignores SIGTERM. The leader calls /team kill, /team shutdown, or stopAllTeammates(). The SIGKILL escalation never fires, so the child process remains alive while the leader thinks it was stopped.

claim: `/team cleanup` deletes the active team directory and then immediately calls refreshTasks(), whose leader-side bootstrap path recreates config.json when it is missing. The cleanup command therefore resurrects the team state it just removed, so cleanup is not persistent.
evidence: leader-lifecycle-commands.ts:241-249 calls cleanupTeamDir(...) and then refreshTasks(). leader.ts:447-456 loads config and falls back to ensureTeamConfig(...) when config.json is missing, recreating the team directory on the next refresh.
runtimeTrigger: Leader runs `/team cleanup --force` on the active team. The directory is removed, but the post-cleanup refresh (and subsequent background refresh loop) recreates config.json immediately, so the supposedly deleted team artifacts reappear.

claim: `/team shutdown` skips manual non-RPC workers that currently own in-progress tasks. Because the loop `continue`s before sending any shutdown_request, those workers keep running even though the command reports that all teammates were stopped.
evidence: leader-lifecycle-commands.ts:379-383 skips manual workers when `inProgressOwners.has(m.name)`, and the shutdown_request mailbox write happens only after that branch. The final notify at 410 still says all teammates stopped.
runtimeTrigger: A tmux/manual worker launched via `/team env` is online and owns an in-progress task. The leader runs `/team shutdown`. RPC workers stop, but the manual worker receives no shutdown_request and keeps running while the UI claims shutdown completed.
